# rpi-deploy

> rpi-deploy builds and runs Docker Compose projects on a Raspberry Pi over plain SSH — no container registry, no Kubernetes, no YAML pipelines. It ships as two binaries: the `rpi` CLI (runs on your machine or CI) and `rpi agent` (a systemd daemon on the Pi that clones the repo, builds the Compose stack, and allocates a stable port).

## Install

```
npm install -g rpi-deploy
```

Prebuilt binaries for Linux and Windows; builds from source on macOS. No Rust toolchain or Docker required on the client machine.

## Quick start

1. `npm install -g rpi-deploy`
2. `sudo rpi agent setup` on the Pi, then `rpi setup` on your machine to save an SSH profile.
3. `rpi init` scaffolds an `rpi.toml` in your project — or write one by hand describing `[project]`, `[source]`, and `[ingress]`.
4. `rpi deploy` — fetches, builds, starts, health-checks, routes, and garbage-collects in one pipeline.

## Key features

- Latest-wins deploy queue: a newer deploy cancels a stale one in flight.
- Encrypted secrets: env files and secret files delivered as sealed bundles.
- Cloudflare Tunnel ingress: a public hostname without opening a port.
- Health checks (HTTP or TCP) gate every deploy.
- Stable port allocation per project across deploys.
- Survives reboots: deployed containers restart automatically after a Pi power-cycle (`restart: unless-stopped`); a deliberate `rpi stop` stays stopped.
- Lifecycle commands: `rpi logs -f`, `rpi stats -w` (live TUI with history sparklines), start/stop/restart.
- One-off commands (migrations, backups) declared in `rpi.toml`, run inside the container.
- Installs in seconds: prebuilt binary for Linux and Windows via `npm install -g` — no toolchain or Docker on your machine (macOS builds from source).
- Live deploy pipeline: fetch → build → start → health → route → gc, each stage collapsing to a timed summary, with a service count in the final stamp.
- Deploy-key preflight: registers a read-only GitHub deploy key for private repos on first deploy.

## Docs

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/khmilevoi/rpi-deploy): source, README, issues.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/rpi-deploy): install and version history.
